records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1284 - It is narrated from Muhammad ibn Isma'il ibn Bazi' from Imam al-Ridha (as) that He said:
I asked him about the prayer in Mecca and Medina, whether it should be shortened or completed.
Imam (as) said: "Shorten it unless you intend to stay for ten days”.
records.arabicText: 1284 - مَا رَوَاهُ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ بَزِيعٍ عَنْ أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ اَلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلصَّلاَةِ بِمَكَّةَ وَ اَلْمَدِينَةِ يُقَصَّرُ أَوْ يُتَمُّ قَالَ «قَصِّرْ مَا لَمْ تَعْزِمْ عَلَى مُقَامِ عَشَرَةِ أَيَّامٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1284 - It is narrated from Muhammad ibn Isma'il ibn Bazi' from Imam al-Ridha (as) that He said:
I asked him about the prayer in Mecca and Medina, whether it should be shortened or completed.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Shorten it unless you intend to stay for ten days”.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1285 - It is narrated from Muhammad ibn Khalid al-Barqi from Hamza ibn Abdullah al-Ja'fari who said:
“When I departed from Mina, I intended to stay in Mecca, so I completed the prayer. Then news came to me from my home, and I found that I had no choice but to return. I was uncertain whether to continue completing my prayers or to shorten them. At that time, Abu al-Hasan (as) was in Mecca, so I approached him and explained the situation.”
Imam (as) said to me: "Return to shortening [your prayers]”.
records.arabicText: 1285 - وَ مَا رَوَاهُ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ خَالِدٍ اَلْبَرْقِيُّ عَنْ حَمْزَةَ بْنِ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ اَلْجَعْفَرِيِّ قَالَ: لَمَّا أَنْ نَفَرْتُ مِنْ مِنًى نَوَيْتُ اَلْمُقَامَ بِمَكَّةَ فَأَتْمَمْتُ اَلصَّلاَةَ ثُمَّ جَاءَنِي خَبَرٌ مِنَ اَلْمَنْزِلِ فَلَمْ أَجِدْ بُدّاً مِنَ اَلْمَصِيرِ إِلَى اَلْمَنْزِلِ فَلَمْ أَدْرِ أُتِمُّ أَمْ أُقَصِّرُ وَ أَبُو اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَوْمَئِذٍ بِمَكَّةَ فَأَتَيْتُهُ فَقَصَصْتُ عَلَيْهِ اَلْقِصَّةَ فَقَالَ لِيَ «اِرْجِعْ إِلَى اَلتَّقْصِيرِ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1285 - It is narrated from Muhammad ibn Khalid al-Barqi from Hamza ibn Abdullah al-Ja'fari who said:
“When I departed from Mina, I intended to stay in Mecca, so I completed the prayer. Then news came to me from my home, and I found that I had no choice but to return. I was uncertain whether to continue completing my prayers or to shorten them. At that time, Abu al-Hasan (as) was in Mecca, so I approached him and explained the situation.”
Imam (as) said to me:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Return to shortening [your prayers]”.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1286 - Al-Fudhayl ibn Yasaar reported from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
"There is no Jumu'ah (Jumu’ah prayer), nor Eid al-Adha, nor Eid al-Fitr for the traveler”.
records.arabicText: 1286 - وروى الفضيل بن يسار، عن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال: " ليس في السفر جمعة ولا أضحى ولا فطر.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1286 - Al-Fudhayl ibn Yasaar reported from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no Jumu'ah (Jumu’ah prayer), nor Eid al-Adha, nor Eid al-Fitr for the traveler”.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1287 - Ismail ibn Jabir reported:
“I said to Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as): "The time for prayer enters while I am traveling, so I do not pray until I return home."
Imam (as) said: "Pray and complete the prayer."
I said: "The time for prayer enters while I am at home and I intend to travel, so I do not pray until I have departed."
Imam (as) said: "Pray and shorten the prayer, for if you do not, you have opposed the Messenger of Allah (sw)”.
records.arabicText: 1287 - وَ رَوَى إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ جَابِرٍ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَدْخُلُ عَلَيَّ وَقْتُ اَلصَّلاَةِ وَ أَنَا فِي اَلسَّفَرِ فَلاَ أُصَلِّي حَتَّى أَدْخُلَ أَهْلِي فَقَالَ «صَلِّ وَ أَتِمَّ اَلصَّلاَةَ» قُلْتُ فَيَدْخُلُ عَلَيَّ وَقْتُ اَلصَّلاَةِ وَ أَنَا فِي أَهْلِي أُرِيدُ اَلسَّفَرَ فَلاَ أُصَلِّي حَتَّى أَخْرُجَ قَالَ «صَلِّ وَ قَصِّرْ فَإِنْ لَمْ تَفْعَلْ فَقَدْ خَالَفْتَ رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1287 - Ismail ibn Jabir reported:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
“I said to Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as): "The time for prayer enters while I am traveling, so I do not pray until I return home."
Imam (as) said: "Pray and complete the prayer."
I said: "The time for prayer enters while I am at home and I intend to travel, so I do not pray until I have departed."
Imam (as) said: "Pray and shorten the prayer, for if you do not, you have opposed the Messenger of Allah (sw)”.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1288 - Regarding the report of Hariz from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), who said:
"I asked him about a man who returns from his journey while the time for prayer has entered, and he is still on the road."
Imam (as) said: "He should pray two rak'ahs (units of prayer). If he sets out on a journey and the time for prayer has already entered, then he should pray four rak'ahs."
records.arabicText: 1288 - وَ أَمَّا خَبَرُ حَرِيزٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ يَدْخُلُ مِنْ سَفَرِهِ وَ قَدْ دَخَلَ وَقْتُ اَلصَّلاَةِ وَ هُوَ فِي اَلطَّرِيقِ قَالَ «يُصَلِّي رَكْعَتَيْنِ وَ إِنْ خَرَجَ إِلَى سَفَرِهِ وَ قَدْ دَخَلَ وَقْتُ اَلصَّلاَةِ فَلْيُصَلِّ أَرْبَعاً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1288 - Regarding the report of Hariz from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I asked him about a man who returns from his journey while the time for prayer has entered, and he is still on the road."
Imam (as) said: "He should pray two rak'ahs (units of prayer). If he sets out on a journey and the time for prayer has already entered, then he should pray four rak'ahs."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1289 - In the book of Al-Hakam ibn Miskin, it is reported that Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about a man who arrives from his journey during the time for a prayer.
Imam (as) said: "If he does not fear that the time for the prayer will expire, he should complete it (in full). But if he fears that the time will expire, he should shorten it”.
records.arabicText: 1289 - فِي كِتَابِ اَلْحَكَمِ بْنِ مِسْكِينٍ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: فِي اَلرَّجُلِ يَقْدَمُ مِنْ سَفَرِهِ فِي وَقْتِ صَلاَةٍ فَقَالَ «إِنْ كَانَ لاَ يَخَافُ خُرُوجَ اَلْوَقْتِ فَلْيُتِمَّ وَ إِنْ كَانَ يَخَافُ خُرُوجَ اَلْوَقْتِ فَلْيُقَصِّرْ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1289 - In the book of Al-Hakam ibn Miskin, it is reported that Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about a man who arrives from his journey during the time for a prayer.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "If he does not fear that the time for the prayer will expire, he should complete it (in full). But if he fears that the time will expire, he should shorten it”.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1290 - Ishaq ibn Ammar asked Abu Ibrahim Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) about a man who is traveling and then arrives, entering the boundaries of Kufa.
He asked: "Should he perform the full prayer or continue to shorten it until he enters his home?"
Imam (as) said: "Rather, he should continue to shorten it until he enters his home”.
records.arabicText: 1290 - وَ سَأَلَ إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ أَبَا إِبْرَاهِيمَ مُوسَى بْنَ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ: فِي اَلرَّجُلِ يَكُونُ مُسَافِراً ثُمَّ يَقْدَمُ فَيَدْخُلُ بُيُوتَ اَلْكُوفَةِ أَ يُتِمُّ اَلصَّلاَةَ أَمْ يَكُونُ مُقَصِّراً حَتَّى يَدْخُلَ إِلَى أَهْلِهِ قَالَ «بَلْ يَكُونُ مُقَصِّراً حَتَّى يَدْخُلَ إِلَى أَهْلِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1290 - Ishaq ibn Ammar asked Abu Ibrahim Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) about a man who is traveling and then arrives, entering the boundaries of Kufa.
He asked:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Should he perform the full prayer or continue to shorten it until he enters his home?"
Imam (as) said: "Rather, he should continue to shorten it until he enters his home”.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1291 - Saif al-Tammar narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that one of our companions said to him:
"We used to make up the daytime prayers when we would stop between Maghrib (evening prayer) and Isha (night prayer)."
He (as) replied: "No, Allah (swt) knows His servants best when He granted the concession. Allah (swt) the Mighty and Majestic has prescribed for the traveler two rak'ahs (units of prayer); there is nothing before or after them except for the night prayer while you are on your mount, wherever it may take you”.
records.arabicText: 1291 - وَ رَوَى سَيْفٌ اَلتَّمَّارُ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: قَالَ لَهُ بَعْضُ أَصْحَابِنَا كُنَّا نَقْضِي صَلاَةَ اَلنَّهَارِ إِذَا نَزَلْنَا بَيْنَ اَلْمَغْرِبِ وَ اَلْعِشَاءِ اَلْآخِرَةِ فَقَالَ «لاَ اَللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِعِبَادِهِ حِينَ رَخَّصَ إِنَّمَا فَرَضَ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ عَلَى اَلْمُسَافِرِ رَكْعَتَيْنِ لاَ قَبْلَهُمَا وَ لاَ بَعْدَهُمَا شَيْءٌ إِلاَّ صَلاَةُ اَللَّيْلِ عَلَى بَعِيرِكَ حَيْثُ تَوَجَّهَ بِكَ.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1292 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about performing the supererogatory (nafilah) prayers during the day while traveling.
Imam (as) said: "If it were appropriate to perform the nafilah (supererogatory) prayers during travel, then the obligatory (fardh) prayers would have been completed (in full)”.
records.arabicText: 1292 - وَ سُئِلَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنْ صَلاَةِ اَلنَّافِلَةِ بِالنَّهَارِ فِي سَفَرٍ فَقَالَ «لَوْ صَلَحَتِ اَلنَّافِلَةُ فِي اَلسَّفَرِ تَمَّتِ اَلْفَرِيضَةُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1292 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about performing the supererogatory (nafilah) prayers during the day while traveling.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If it were appropriate to perform the nafilah (supererogatory) prayers during travel, then the obligatory (fardh) prayers would have been completed (in full)”.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.1293 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) would perform the obligatory prayer on his mount on a rainy day.
records.arabicText: 1293 - وَ كَانَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ يُصَلِّي عَلَى رَاحِلَتِهِ اَلْفَرِيضَةَ فِي يَوْمٍ مَطِيرٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1293 -
records.thaqalaynMatn: The Messenger of Allah (sw) would perform the obligatory prayer on his mount on a rainy day.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1294 - Ibrahim al-Karkhi said:
"I said to Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as): "I am able to face the Qiblah (direction of prayer) while in the mahmil (a covered mount or carrier)."
He (as) replied: "Is this restriction (truly necessary) ? Do you not have in the Messenger of Allah (sw) an example to follow?"
records.arabicText: 1294 - وَقَالَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ اَلْكَرْخِيُّ: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ إِنِّي أَقْدِرُ أَنْ أَتَوَجَّهَ نَحْوَ اَلْقِبْلَةِ فِي اَلْمَحْمِلِ فَقَالَ «هَذَا اَلضِّيقُ أَ مَا لَكُمْ فِي رَسُولِ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ أُسْوَةٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1294 - Ibrahim al-Karkhi said:
"I said to Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I am able to face the Qiblah (direction of prayer) while in the mahmil (a covered mount or carrier)."
He (as) replied: "Is this restriction (truly necessary) ? Do you not have in the Messenger of Allah (sw) an example to follow?"

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1295 - Sa'd ibn Sa'd asked Abu al-Hasan al-Ridha (as) about a man who is with a menstruating woman in a mahmil (covered mount or carrier).
He asked: "Should he pray while She is with him?"
He (as) replied: "Yes”.
records.arabicText: 1295 - وَ سَأَلَ سَعْدُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ اَلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ تَكُونُ مَعَهُ اَلْمَرْأَةُ اَلْحَائِضُ فِي اَلْمَحْمِلِ أَ يُصَلِّي وَ هِيَ مَعَهُ قَالَ «نَعَمْ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1295 - Sa'd ibn Sa'd asked Abu al-Hasan al-Ridha (as) about a man who is with a menstruating woman in a mahmil (covered mount or carrier).
He asked:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Should he pray while She is with him?"
He (as) replied: "Yes”.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1296 - Sa'id ibn Yasarasked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a man performing the night prayer while on his mount.
He asked: "Is it permissible for him to cover his face while praying?"
He (as) replied: "Yes, when he is reciting (the Qur'an). However, when he gestures with his face for prostration, he should uncover it wherever the mount takes him”.
records.arabicText: 1296 - وَ سَأَلَ سَعِيدُ بْنُ يَسَارٍ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يُصَلِّي صَلاَةَ اَللَّيْلِ وَ هُوَ عَلَى دَابَّتِهِ أَ لَهُ أَنْ يُغَطِّيَ وَجْهَهُ وَ هُوَ يُصَلِّي قَالَ «أَمَّا إِذَا قَرَأَ فَنَعَمْ وَ أَمَّا إِذَا أَوْمَأَ بِوَجْهِهِ لِلسُّجُودِ فَلْيَكْشِفْهُ حَيْثُ مَا أَوْمَأَتْ بِهِ اَلدَّابَّةُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1296 - Sa'id ibn Yasarasked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a man performing the night prayer while on his mount.
He asked:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Is it permissible for him to cover his face while praying?"
He (as) replied: "Yes, when he is reciting (the Qur'an). However, when he gestures with his face for prostration, he should uncover it wherever the mount takes him”.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1297 - Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a man performing supererogatory (nafilah) prayers in towns and cities while on his mount, wherever it faces.
He (as) replied: "There is no harm."
records.arabicText: 1297 - وَ سَأَلَ عَبْدُ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ اَلْحَجَّاجِ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يُصَلِّي اَلنَّوَافِلَ فِي اَلْأَمْصَارِ وَ هُوَ عَلَى دَابَّتِهِ حَيْثُمَا تَوَجَّهَتْ بِهِ قَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1297 - Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a man performing supererogatory (nafilah) prayers in towns and cities while on his mount, wherever it faces.
records.thaqalaynMatn: He (as) replied: "There is no harm."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1298 - Ali ibn Yaqtin asked Abu al-Hasan (as) about a man who sets out on a journey and then decides to stay (reside) while he is in the midst of prayer.
Imam (as) said: "He should complete (the full prayer) when he decides to stay."
He also asked about a man who accompanies his brother to the point where shortening prayers and breaking the fast becomes obligatory.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in that."
records.arabicText:
1298 - وَ سَأَلَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ يَقْطِينٍ ، أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَخْرُجُ فِي اَلسَّفَرِ ثُمَّ يَبْدُو لَهُ فِي اَلْإِقَامَةِ وَ هُوَ فِي اَلصَّلاَةِ قَالَ «يُتِمُّ إِذَا بَدَتْ لَهُ اَلْإِقَامَةُ»
وَ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يُشَيِّعُ أَخَاهُ إِلَى اَلْمَكَانِ اَلَّذِي يَجِبُ عَلَيْهِ فِيهِ اَلتَّقْصِيرُ وَ اَلْإِفْطَارُ قَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ بِذَلِكَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1298 - Ali ibn Yaqtin asked Abu al-Hasan (as) about a man who sets out on a journey and then decides to stay (reside) while he is in the midst of prayer.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"He should complete (the full prayer) when he decides to stay."
He also asked about a man who accompanies his brother to the point where shortening prayers and breaking the fast becomes obligatory.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in that."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1299 - In a narration from Abu Basir, Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"You are in the time of Maghrib (evening prayer) while traveling up to a distance of five miles after sunset”.
records.arabicText: 1299 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «أَنْتَ فِي وَقْتِ اَلْمَغْرِبِ فِي اَلسَّفَرِ إِلَى خَمْسَةِ أَمْيَالٍ مِنْ بَعْدِ غُرُوبِ اَلشَّمْسِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1299 - In a narration from Abu Basir, Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "You are in the time of Maghrib (evening prayer) while traveling up to a distance of five miles after sunset”.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1300 - Ammar al-Sabati asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about the extent of wetness or mud that makes it unsuitable for prostration.
Imam (as) said: "When the forehead sinks into it and does not remain firm on the ground”.
records.arabicText: 1300 - وَ سَأَلَ عَمَّارٌ اَلسَّابَاطِيُّ ، أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنْ حَدِّ اَلطِّينِ اَلَّذِي لاَ يُسْجَدُ فِيهِ مَا هُوَقَالَ «إِذَا غَرِقَتْ فِيهِ اَلْجَبْهَةُ وَ لَمْ تَثْبُتْ عَلَى اَلْأَرْضِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1300 - Ammar al-Sabati asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about the extent of wetness or mud that makes it unsuitable for prostration.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "When the forehead sinks into it and does not remain firm on the ground”.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1301 - Muawiya ibn Ammar said to Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as): "The people of Mecca complete (perform in full) their prayers at Arafat."
He (as) replied: "Woe to them! (Or, Alas for them!) What journey is more significant than this? No, it should not be completed”.
records.arabicText: 1301 - وَقَالَ مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: إِنَّ أَهْلَ مَكَّةَ يُتِمُّونَ اَلصَّلاَةَ بِعَرَفَاتٍ ، قَالَ «وَيْلَهُمْ أَوْ وَيْحَهُمْ وَ أَيُّ سَفَرٍ أَشَدُّ مِنْهُ لاَ لاَ يُتَمُّ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1301 - Muawiya ibn Ammar said to Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"The people of Mecca complete (perform in full) their prayers at Arafat."
He (as) replied: "Woe to them! (Or, Alas for them!) What journey is more significant than this? No, it should not be completed”.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1302 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"When Archangel Jibril (as) descended upon the Messenger of Allah (sw) with the ruling of shortening (the prayer), the Prophet (sw) asked him, 'For how long (a distance) is this required?'
Archangel Jibril (as) replied: 'For a barid.'
The Prophet (sw) asked: 'And what is a barid?'
Jibril said: 'The distance between the shadow of 'Ayr to the shade of Wu'ayr.'
The Banu Umayyah measured it, divided it into twelve miles, with each mile being one thousand five hundred cubits, equating to four farsakhs (parasangs).'"
records.arabicText: 1302 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «إِنَّ رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ لَمَّا نَزَلَ عَلَيْهِ جَبْرَئِيلُ بِالتَّقْصِيرِ قَالَ لَهُ اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ «فِي كَمْ ذَلِكَ» فَقَالَ فِي بَرِيدٍ قَالَ «وَ كَمِ اَلْبَرِيدُ» قَالَ مَا بَيْنَ ظِلِّ عَيْرٍ إِلَى فَيْءِ وُعَيْرٍ فَذَرَعَتْهُ بَنُو أُمَيَّةَ ثُمَّ جَزَّءُوهُ عَلَى اِثْنَيْ عَشَرَ مِيلاً فَكَانَ كُلُّ مِيلٍ أَلْفاً وَ خَمْسَمِائَةِ ذِرَاعٍ وَ هُوَ أَرْبَعَةُ فَرَاسِخَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1302 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"When Archangel Jibril (as) descended upon the Messenger of Allah (sw) with the ruling of shortening (the prayer), the Prophet (sw) asked him, 'For how long (a distance) is this required?'
Archangel Jibril (as) replied: 'For a barid.'
The Prophet (sw) asked: 'And what is a barid?'
Jibril said: 'The distance between the shadow of 'Ayr to the shade of Wu'ayr.'
The Banu Umayyah measured it, divided it into twelve miles, with each mile being one thousand five hundred cubits, equating to four farsakhs (parasangs).'"

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1303 - Jamil ibn Darraj reported from Zurarah ibn A'yan, who said:
I asked Abu Ja'far (as) about the ruling for shortening the prayer.
Imam (as) said: "A barid going and a barid returning. The Messenger of Allah (sw) would shorten his prayers when he reached Dhu'bāb."
records.arabicText: 1303 - خَبَرُ جَمِيلِ بْنِ دَرَّاجٍ عَنْ زُرَارَةَ بْنِ أَعْيَنَ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلتَّقْصِيرِ فَقَالَ «بَرِيدٌ ذَاهِبٌ وَ بَرِيدٌ جَائِي وَ كَانَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ إِذَا أَتَى ذُبَاباً قَصَّرَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1303 - Jamil ibn Darraj reported from Zurarah ibn A'yan, who said:
I asked Abu Ja'far (as) about the ruling for shortening the prayer.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A barid going and a barid returning. The Messenger of Allah (sw) would shorten his prayers when he reached Dhu'bāb."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1304 - Zakariyya ibn Adam asked Abu al-Hasan al-Ridha (as) about the distance for shortening prayers when a man is in the lands belonging to his family and has control over them, traveling through these lands for two days and two nights or three days and their nights.
Abu al-Hasan (as) wrote in response, "Shortening is applicable for the journey of one day and one night."
records.arabicText: 1304 - وَ سَأَلَ زَكَرِيَّا بْنُ آدَمَ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ اَلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلتَّقْصِيرِ فِي كَمْ يُقَصِّرُ اَلرَّجُلُ إِذَا كَانَ فِي ضِيَاعِ أَهْلِ بَيْتِهِ وَ أَمْرُهُ جَائِزٌ فِيهَا يَسِيرُ فِي اَلضِّيَاعِ يَوْمَيْنِ وَ لَيْلَتَيْنِ وَ ثَلاَثَةَ أَيَّامٍ وَ لَيَالِيَهُنَّ فَكَتَبَ «اَلتَّقْصِيرُ فِي مَسِيرَةِ يَوْمٍ وَ لَيْلَةٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1304 - Zakariyya ibn Adam asked Abu al-Hasan al-Ridha (as) about the distance for shortening prayers when a man is in the lands belonging to his family and has control over them, traveling through these lands for two days and two nights or three days and their nights.
Abu al-Hasan (as) wrote in response,
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Shortening is applicable for the journey of one day and one night."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1305 - Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr reported from Muhammad ibn Ishaq ibn Ammar, who said:
I asked Abu al-Hasan al-Ridha (as) about a woman who was on the way to Mecca and performed the Maghrib prayer two rak'ahs going and two rak'ahs returning.
Imam (as) said: "She does not need to repeat it."
In another narration from al-Hussain ibn Sa'id, from ibn Abi Umayr, from Muhammad ibn Ishaq ibn Ammar, from Abu al-Hasan (as), Imam (as) said:
"There is no obligation to make it up."
records.arabicText:
1305 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ اَلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اِمْرَأَةٍ كَانَتْ فِي طَرِيقِ مَكَّةَ فَصَلَّتْ ذَاهِبَةً وَ جَائِيَةً اَلْمَغْرِبَ رَكْعَتَيْنِ رَكْعَتَيْنِ فَقَالَ «لَيْسَ عَلَيْهَا إِعَادَةٌ.
وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ اَلْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنِ اِبْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «لَيْسَ عَلَيْهَا قَضَاءٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1305 - Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr reported from Muhammad ibn Ishaq ibn Ammar, who said:
I asked Abu al-Hasan al-Ridha (as) about a woman who was on the way to Mecca and performed the Maghrib prayer two rak'ahs going and two rak'ahs returning.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"She does not need to repeat it."
In another narration from al-Hussain ibn Sa'id, from ibn Abi Umayr, from Muhammad ibn Ishaq ibn Ammar, from Abu al-Hasan (as), Imam (as) said:
"There is no obligation to make it up."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1306 - In a narration from Al-‘Ala from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), He said:
"If a traveler prays behind residents (those who are not traveling), he should complete his prayer as two rak'ahs and then give the salutation (end the prayer).
If he prays with them at Dhuhr (noon prayer), he should make the first two rak'ahs for Dhuhr and the last two for Asr (afternoon prayer)."
[REFERENCES]
Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 451 • Wasail Us Shia, Vol. 8, p. 329 • Wasail Us Shia, Vol. 8, p. 400
records.arabicText: 1306 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ اَلْعَلاَءِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «إِذَا صَلَّى اَلْمُسَافِرُ خَلْفَ قَوْمٍ حُضُورٍ فَلْيُتِمَّ صَلاَتَهُ رَكْعَتَيْنِ وَ يُسَلِّمُ وَ إِنْ صَلَّى مَعَهُمُ اَلظُّهْرَ فَلْيَجْعَلِ اَلْأَوَّلَتَيْنِ اَلظُّهْرَ وَ اَلْأَخِيرَتَيْنِ اَلْعَصْرَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1306 - In a narration from Al-‘Ala from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"If a traveler prays behind residents (those who are not traveling), he should complete his prayer as two rak'ahs and then give the salutation (end the prayer).
If he prays with them at Dhuhr (noon prayer), he should make the first two rak'ahs for Dhuhr and the last two for Asr (afternoon prayer)."
[REFERENCES]
Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 451 • Wasail Us Shia, Vol. 8, p. 329 • Wasail Us Shia, Vol. 8, p. 400

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1307 - Ismail ibn al-Fadl asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a man who travels from one land to another but resides in his own villages and properties.
Imam (as) said: "If you stay in your own village or land, then complete the prayer; if you are in land that is not yours, then shorten the prayer."
records.arabicText: 1307 - وَ سَأَلَ إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ اَلْفَضْلِ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنْ رَجُلٍ يُسَافِرُ مِنْ أَرْضٍ إِلَى أَرْضٍ وَ إِنَّمَا يَنْزِلُ قُرَاهُ وَ ضَيْعَتَهُ فَقَالَ «إِذَا نَزَلْتَ قُرَاكَ وَ أَرْضَكَ فَأَتِمَّ اَلصَّلاَةَ وَ إِذَا كُنْتَ فِي غَيْرِ أَرْضِكَ فَقَصِّرْ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1307 - Ismail ibn al-Fadl asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a man who travels from one land to another but resides in his own villages and properties.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If you stay in your own village or land, then complete the prayer; if you are in land that is not yours, then shorten the prayer."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1308 - Muhammad ibn Isma'il ibn Bazi' reported from Abu al-Hasan al-Ridha (as) that he asked about a man shortening his prayers at his property.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm as long as he does not intend to stay for ten days, unless he has a house there where he resides."
I asked him, "What is meant by 'resides'?"
Imam (as) said: "It means he has a house where he stays for six months. If that is the case, he should complete (his prayers) whenever he enters it."
records.arabicText: 1308 - مَا رَوَاهُ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ بَزِيعٍ عَنْ أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ اَلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يُقَصِّرُ فِي ضَيْعَتِهِ فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ مَا لَمْ يَنْوِ مُقَامَ عَشَرَةِ أَيَّامٍ إِلاَّ أَنْ يَكُونَ لَهُ بِهَا مَنْزِلٌ يَسْتَوْطِنُهُ» قَالَ قُلْتُ لَهُ مَا اَلاِسْتِيطَانُ فَقَالَ «أَنْ يَكُونَ لَهُ بِهَا مَنْزِلٌ يُقِيمُ فِيهِ سِتَّةَ أَشْهُرٍ فَإِذَا كَانَ كَذَلِكَ يُتِمُّ فِيهَا مَتَى دَخَلَهَا.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1308 - Muhammad ibn Isma'il ibn Bazi' reported from Abu al-Hasan al-Ridha (as) that he asked about a man shortening his prayers at his property.
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm as long as he does not intend to stay for ten days, unless he has a house there where he resides."
I asked him, "What is meant by 'resides'?"
Imam (as) said: "It means he has a house where he stays for six months. If that is the case, he should complete (his prayers) whenever he enters it."
